samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. 6 8 Counter for the write operation. Counter for the read operation.

Define a register array, RAM, with a depth of DEPTH and a width of WIDTH, initialized to 0.

reg [DEPTH - 1 : 0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];

Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

On reset, clear all the bits in the RAM array to 0.

When write_en is 1, perform a write operation to the RAM array at the specified write_addr with the specified write_data.

Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

On reset, clear the read_data to 0.

When read_en is 1, read the data from the RAM array at the specified read_addr to the read_data.
